restaurant.name: Yene Habeshan Food & Catering
restaurant.tagline: Authentic Habeshan Taste Delivered to You
restaurant.description: Authentic Habeshan flavors delivered to your door. We offer delivery and catering services — bringing the taste of home to your table.

items.title: Doro Wat
items.description: The most beloved Habeshan dish — slow-cooked chicken in rich berbere spice sauce with boiled eggs.
items.recipe:
Ingredients:
- 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces
- 4 boiled eggs
- 4 onions, finely chopped
- 4 tbsp berbere spice
- 3 tbsp niter kibbeh (spiced butter)
- 1 tsp cardamom
- Salt to taste

Method:
Dry-cook onions until golden. Add niter kibbeh and berbere, cook 10 min. Add chicken, coat well. Add water, simmer 45 min. Add scored boiled eggs last 10 min.

items.title: Misir Wat
items.description: Spiced red lentils slow-cooked with berbere, onions, and niter kibbeh. A vegan classic.
items.recipe:
Ingredients:
- 2 cups red lentils
- 3 onions, diced
- 3 tbsp berbere
- 2 tbsp niter kibbeh
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1 tsp ginger
- Salt to taste

Method:
Sauté onions until caramelised. Add garlic, ginger, berbere. Add lentils and water, simmer 30 min until thick.

items.title: Tibs (Beef)
items.description: Sautéed tender beef with rosemary, jalapeño, onions, and Habeshan spices. Served sizzling.
items.recipe:
Ingredients:
- 500g beef, cubed
- 2 onions, sliced
- 2 jalapeños
- 2 sprigs rosemary
- 2 tbsp niter kibbeh
- 1 tsp mitmita
- Salt & pepper

Method:
Heat niter kibbeh in pan. Sear beef on high heat. Add onions, jalapeño, rosemary. Season with mitmita. Serve immediately.

items.title: Injera
items.description: Traditional sourdough flatbread made from teff flour. The foundation of every Habeshan meal.
items.recipe:
Ingredients:
- 2 cups teff flour
- 2.5 cups water
- Pinch of salt

Method:
Mix teff and water, ferment 2–3 days. Add salt, pour thin batter onto hot flat pan. Cover and cook until bubbles form and surface is dry. Do not flip.

items.title: Shiro Wat
items.description: Smooth chickpea flour stew with berbere and niter kibbeh. A comforting Habeshan staple.
items.recipe:
Ingredients:
- 1 cup shiro powder (chickpea flour blend)
- 3 onions
- 3 tbsp berbere
- 2 tbsp niter kibbeh
- 3 cups water
- Garlic & ginger

Method:
Sauté onions, add berbere and niter kibbeh. Gradually whisk in shiro powder with water. Simmer 20 min, stirring constantly.

items.title: Kitfo
items.description: Habeshan-style minced beef seasoned with mitmita and niter kibbeh. Served raw, medium, or well-done.
items.recipe:
Ingredients:
- 500g lean beef mince
- 2 tbsp niter kibbeh
- 1 tsp mitmita
- 1 tsp cardamom
- Salt to taste

Method:
Mix warm niter kibbeh with mitmita and cardamom. Combine with beef mince. Serve as desired — raw (tere), lightly warmed (lebleb), or cooked (yebesele).

items.title: Beyaynetu (Fasting Platter)
items.description: A colourful vegan platter of assorted Habeshan dishes on injera — misir, shiro, gomen, and more.
items.recipe:
Includes:
- Misir wat (red lentils)
- Shiro wat (chickpea stew)
- Gomen (collard greens)
- Tikil gomen (cabbage & carrots)
- Fosolia (green beans)
- Served on injera

Perfect for fasting days or vegan guests.

items.title: Tej (Honey Wine)
items.description: Traditional Habeshan honey wine with a light, sweet, and slightly bitter taste.
items.recipe: Traditional fermented honey wine. Served chilled in a berele (flask-shaped glass).

items.title: Habeshan Coffee Ceremony
items.description: Freshly roasted and brewed Habeshan coffee served in three rounds — abol, tona, and baraka.
items.recipe: Green coffee beans roasted fresh, ground by hand, brewed in a jebena (clay pot). Served with popcorn and incense. Three rounds: Abol (first), Tona (second), Baraka (blessing).

items.title: Catering Package
items.description: Full Habeshan feast for events — injera, assorted wats, tibs, and drinks. Serves 10–12.
items.recipe:
Package includes:
- Large injera rolls
- Doro wat
- Misir wat
- Beef tibs
- Beyaynetu platter
- Tej or soft drinks
- Disposable serving ware

Minimum 48h advance booking required.
